etymology.description: Depicts a stylized footprint pointing upward -> originally meaning 'foot' -> later forms like 趾(趾) (zhǐ, 'toe') refer back to original meaning
etymology.interpretation: 龰(龰) (yù, 'stylized feet') is a pictogram showing a footprint pointing up, originally indicating the concept of a foot. The character 趾(趾) (zhǐ, 'toe') evolved from this and retains the reference to the original meaning. It visually resembles a sole with three toes, similar to representations of the human hand in characters like 爪(爪) (zhuǎ, 'claw'), 寸(寸) (cùn, 'inch'), and 又(又) (yòu, 'again').
